title: QRC — Quick Reaction Checklists
summary: The QRC module provides Airfield Managers with 25 configurable emergency and contingency checklists, supporting 8 step types, per-execution progress tracking, annual template review, and per-user monthly review compliance.
concept:
Quick Reaction Checklists (QRCs) are the digital equivalent of the laminated emergency cards that Airfield Management personnel reach for when an aircraft declares an emergency, fuel spill occurs, or another contingency arises. Glidepath supports up to 25 QRCs per base, each stored as a template (the static checklist definition) and instantiated into an execution (the live, in-progress instance with per-step responses) every time an Airfield Manager activates one.

Data model

  • qrc_templates — one row per checklist. Stores qrc_number (1–25), title, steps (JSONB tree), optional has_scn_form flag + scn_fields JSONB, and annual review metadata (last_reviewed_at, last_reviewed_by, review_notes).
  • qrc_executions — one row per opened checklist instance. status is 'open' or 'closed'. step_responses is a JSONB map keyed by step.id. scn_data holds SCN form field values. label is the optional manual identifier.
  • qrc_monthly_reviews — one row per user per template per review event. Snapshots template_updated_at_at_review to prove which version was reviewed.

Eight step types

TypeBehavior
checkboxToggle: undefined → completed → N/A → undefined
checkbox_with_noteSame toggle + free-text note field
notify_agenciesPer-agency sub-toggles (each can be completed or N/A independently)
fill_fieldFree text input; auto-marks step completed when non-empty; first non-empty value drives the active-list identifier
time_fieldHHMM text input + "Now (Z)" button that stamps current Zulu time
conditionalInline instruction rendered in amber italic — not checkable
textInformational block with cyan left border — not checkable
textareaMulti-line informational block — not checkable

Active-list identifier — deriveQrcIdentifier

When multiple QRCs of the same type are open simultaneously (e.g. two simultaneous emergencies), the active list needs a way to tell them apart. deriveQrcIdentifier in lib/qrc/identifier.ts resolves this with a four-step priority:

  1. A manual label the Airfield Manager typed into the label field wins outright.
  2. For SCN-form QRCs: the call_sign SCN field (plus type_of_aircraft if filled).
  3. The first fill_field step (depth-first order) that has a non-empty response value.
  4. Empty string — caller renders the row without a sub-label.

Monthly and annual review compliance

QRC templates must be reviewed on a monthly or quarterly cadence (configurable per base via bases.qrc_review_interval). The Reviews tab shows per-template status: Never (no review on record), Overdue (review window elapsed), Updated (template was edited since the last review — needs re-review), or Current. Each qrc_monthly_reviews insert snapshots template_updated_at_at_review so the record proves exactly which version the operator reviewed. A separate annual template review (stored on the template itself) is exposed inside the execution view as the "Annual Review" panel.

PDF and email export

Closed executions can be exported as a PDF via lib/qrc-pdf.ts (returns { doc, filename } per the PDF generator convention) or emailed through the EmailPdfModal component, which calls sendPdfViaEmail() → /api/send-pdf-email (Resend). The monthly review compliance report is a separate PDF generated by lib/qrc-monthly-review-pdf.ts.

SCN integration

QRC templates with has_scn_form = true display a dedicated "Secondary Crash Net (SCN) Form" panel inside the execution view. Field definitions come from the template's scn_fields.fields[] array ({ key, label, type }). Values are saved to qrc_executions.scn_data. The activity log entry on open/close writes "SECONDARY CRASH NET ACTIVATED" instead of the standard QRC open/close text.

code.caption: deriveQrcIdentifier — 4-step active-list label resolution
code.note: Small enough to read in full; shows the depth-first flattenSteps walk and the SCN call_sign priority.

code.caption: QRC data layer — execution lifecycle and step-response merge
code.note: updateStepResponse merges a single step's response into the JSONB map and fills in completed_by attribution if missing.

annotations.note: Attribution fallback: completed_by is stamped client-side for instant display, but if the session wasn't resolved yet this server-side path catches it.

code.caption: closeQrcExecution — status flip, SCN field denormalization, activity log
code.note: On close, SCN field values are appended to the activity log detail string so the Events Log contains the full SCN activation record.

annotations.note: labelMap resolves scn_fields keys to human labels for the activity log — keeps the log readable without a JOIN.

code.caption: QrcExecutionView — 8-type step renderer
code.note: Each step type renders its own UI branch. Sub-steps recurse with depth+1 so the indent clears the parent's number column.

fits: The QRC module relies on the same Supabase data layer patterns used across the app: createClient() null-check guard, friendlyError() for human-readable RLS/constraint errors, and logActivity() for Events Log entries. The useInstallation() hook provides installationId for all base-scoped queries. The sidebar badge count uses fetchActiveQrcCount() (a count: exact, head: true query) combined with the glidepath:badges-refresh custom event to stay current without tight polling.

quiz.q: An Airfield Manager opens QRC-7 twice simultaneously for two different aircraft emergencies. What determines the label shown next to each execution in the Active list?
choices: The qrc_number field, which is unique per template
The deriveQrcIdentifier function: manual label → SCN call_sign → first fill_field value → empty string
The opened_at timestamp formatted as HHmmZ
The initials stored in open_initials on the execution row
quiz.explain: deriveQrcIdentifier in lib/qrc/identifier.ts applies a 4-step resolution: manual label wins, then SCN call_sign (for SCN-form QRCs), then the first non-empty fill_field response, then empty string. This is zero-config — it survives template edits without any label configuration.

quiz.q: An Airfield Manager marks step 3 as 'completed' immediately after opening a new QRC execution before the user session has fully resolved. What happens to the completed_by attribution?
choices: The step is saved with completed_by = null and cannot be attributed later
The page waits for the session before allowing any step actions
The page stamps completed_by for instant display; updateStepResponse fills it server-side if it was missing at the time of the click
The step status is set to not_applicable as a fallback
quiz.explain: updateStepResponse in lib/supabase/qrc.ts has an attribution fallback (lines 248-252): if the response is actioned but completed_by is absent, it calls auth.getUser() to resolve the id and adds it before writing. The page also stamps it optimistically for the rendered completion stamp.

quiz.q: A QRC template has has_scn_form = true and is closed with scn_data = { call_sign: 'REACH123', type_of_aircraft: 'C-17' }. What does the activity log entry look like?
choices: QRC #N COMPLETED — TITLE
SECONDARY CRASH NET ACTIVATED — QRC #N TITLE. CALL_SIGN: REACH123; TYPE_OF_AIRCRAFT: C-17
QRC #N CLOSED — SCN form values are not included in the activity log
SECONDARY CRASH NET ACTIVATED — CALL_SIGN: REACH123 only; aircraft type is excluded
quiz.explain: closeQrcExecution prefixes the detail string with 'SECONDARY CRASH NET ACTIVATED' for SCN-form QRCs, then appends each filled scn_data entry as 'LABEL: VALUE' using the labelMap built from scn_fields.fields[]. Both call_sign and type_of_aircraft are included if non-empty.

title: PPR — Prior Permission Required
summary: The PPR module manages the full lifecycle of Prior Permission Required requests — from public submission through AMOPS triage, multi-agency coordination, and final approval — with email notifications at each stage and a .mil-safe email strategy.
concept:
Prior Permission Required (PPR) is an airfield management control tool: aircraft that are not permanently based at a military airfield must request and receive permission before landing. Glidepath's PPR module handles the full lifecycle in a single place — public requesters submit via a QR-code-accessible form, AMOPS triages and routes for coordination, and an Airfield Manager approves or denies.

PPR status flow

StatusMeaning
pending_amops_triageJust received (public submission or manually entered without pre-coordination); AMOPS must decide routing
pending_coordinationSent to one or more agencies; waiting for concur/non-concur responses
pending_amops_approvalAll coordination complete; Airfield Manager must approve or deny
approvedApproved; PPR number issued to requester
deniedDenied with reason
canceledCanceled after approval

Public submission — SECURITY DEFINER RPC

The public form at /[icao]/ppr-request (and the legacy /ppr-request/[baseId]) is fully unauthenticated. It calls one of two SECURITY DEFINER RPCs: get_public_ppr_config_by_icao to load the base config (columns marked show_on_form = true), then submit_public_ppr_request(p_base_id, ...) to write the entry. This design keeps anon users entirely out of the ppr_entries table — the RPC runs with elevated privileges and enforces its own validation. After the RPC succeeds, the form fires a best-effort call to /api/send-ppr-confirmation, which sends a Resend email to the requester. Importantly, the confirmation email does not include the PPR number — the entry hasn't been triaged yet — and does not contain any deep links to glidepathops.com, because Defender for Office 365 quarantines external hyperlinks from commercial senders on .mil networks.

PPR number format

PPR numbers are minted server-side by the _ppr_generate_number RPC, which uses an atomic counter table to prevent duplicate numbers on concurrent submissions. The format is JJJ-SSS-OI (Julian date – sequence – operating initials). Public submissions use XX as a placeholder OI because there is no authenticated user. On approval, rewritePprOiSegment() replaces the XX segment with the approver's actual operating initials.

Triage — two paths

  • Pre-coordinated path (agencyIds = []): Airfield Manager triages with no agencies selected. The entry jumps directly to approved, fully_certified_at is set, and the approval email fires immediately from triagePprEntry.
  • Coordination path (agencyIds = [...]): A ppr_coordination row is inserted per agency (with status = 'pending'), the entry becomes pending_coordination, and coordination-request emails are sent to each agency's members.

Coordination auto-advance

When an agency member submits their concur/non-concur response, coordinatePprEntry() updates their coordination row, then counts remaining status = 'pending' rows. If the count reaches zero, the entry automatically advances to pending_amops_approval — no manual intervention required. Coordination comments are mirrored into the ppr_remarks table as [AgencyName — CONCUR/NON-CONCUR] comment so the remarks thread is the single human-readable timeline.

Email strategy and .mil deliverability

All PPR emails are sent via Resend from info@glidepathops.com. Deep links are omitted from every email body — https://glidepathops.com/... links are quarantined by Defender for Office 365 until the domain is on the recipient's Safe Links allowlist. The only external link allowed is mailto:. The buildAgencyEmail() helper in lib/ppr-agency-notify.ts uses literal hex colors instead of CSS variables because recipient mail clients cannot resolve CSS custom properties.

Column system

PPR fields are fully configurable per base via ppr_columns. Each column has visibility flags: show_on_form (public submission), show_on_log (AMOPS table), show_on_status (Airfield Status board). formatPprColumnValue() is the single display source of truth — it handles yes_no_na, date, time (with optional local-tz conversion), and fall-through for text types. The "summary columns" optimization (isSummaryColumn) surfaces Callsign and Aircraft Type in slim tables regardless of sort order.

isActivePpr helper

The header chip count and the Airfield Status "Today's PPRs" panel use isActivePpr(status), which returns true for every status except denied and canceled. A denied or canceled request is not on the field and must not inflate the operational count.

code.caption: Public submission flow — config RPC, submit_public_ppr_request, confirmation email
code.note: Shows the full submit path: validation, SECURITY DEFINER RPC call, best-effort confirmation email, and the client-side 5-minute cooldown key.

code.caption: triagePprEntry — pre-coordinated vs. coordination routing with OI rewrite
code.note: The skip path (agencyIds empty) fires an approval email immediately. The coordination path inserts ppr_coordination rows and triggers coordination-request emails.

annotations.note: Pre-coordinated skip path: status → approved, triaged_by + approval_user_id both set to current user, OI segment rewritten, approval email fired.
Coordination path: agency names fetched for denormalized insert, entry → pending_coordination, coordination emails sent best-effort.

code.caption: coordinatePprEntry — auto-advance to pending_amops_approval when last pending row resolves
code.note: Counts remaining pending coordination rows after each agency response; advances status automatically when the count reaches zero.

annotations.note: The idempotent guard (.eq('status', 'pending_coordination')) prevents a race condition where two agency responses arrive simultaneously.
Coordination comment mirrored into ppr_remarks with [Agency — DECISION] prefix so the remarks thread is a complete, single-source timeline.

code.caption: send-ppr-confirmation — .mil-safe confirmation email with no deep links or PPR number
code.note: Demonstrates the .mil email strategy: no https:// links in body, PPR number withheld until approval, rate-limited per requester email and IP.

fits: PPR integrates tightly with the permission matrix: PPR_TRIAGE, PPR_COORDINATE, PPR_APPROVE, PPR_WRITE, and PPR_DELETE are checked via usePermissions() at the top of the page component. The ppr_agencies and ppr_agency_members tables extend the module with a configurable coordination roster: agencies are created in base settings and their members (Supabase users with the agency coordinator role) receive emails when coordination is requested. The fetchPendingTriageCount() and fetchPendingApprovalCount() helpers drive the sidebar badge dot count for AMOPS and Airfield Manager roles respectively.

quiz.q: A public requester submits a PPR via the QR form. Their email arrives in AMOPS, but there is no PPR number in the message. Why?
choices: The PPR number is only minted after approval so the confirmation email intentionally omits it
The send-ppr-confirmation route failed silently and never sent the number
The PPR number is in the email but was stripped by Defender for Office 365
The public form does not support PPR number generation — only AMOPS can create numbered PPRs
quiz.explain: The confirmation email from /api/send-ppr-confirmation deliberately withholds the PPR number (see the route's JSDoc comment). The entry has not been triaged or approved yet. The first email that includes the PPR number is the approval email sent by /api/send-ppr-approval after the Airfield Manager approves.

quiz.q: An AMOPS Airfield Manager triages a public PPR with agencyIds = []. What is the resulting status and what side effects occur?
choices: Status becomes pending_coordination; coordination-request emails are sent to all configured agencies
Status stays pending_amops_triage; the entry is flagged for manual review
Status becomes approved; approval_user_id is set to the current user, OI is rewritten from XX, and the approval email is sent immediately
Status becomes pending_amops_approval; the Airfield Manager must approve in a second step
quiz.explain: When agencyIds is empty, triagePprEntry takes the 'skip' path: status → approved, triaged_by = triaged_at = user + now, approval_user_id = user, OI segment rewritten from XX to the approver's operating initials, and the approval email is fired via /api/send-ppr-approval. This is the pre-coordinated path.

quiz.q: Why does buildAgencyEmail use inline hex colors like '#22c55e' instead of CSS variables like 'var(--color-success)'?
choices: CSS variables are not supported in jsPDF and the same builder is reused for PDF generation
Recipient email clients cannot resolve CSS custom properties, so theme-independent hex values are required for consistent rendering in any inbox
The --color-success variable resolves differently in dark mode and would show the wrong color on mobile
Resend strips all CSS variable references before delivery
quiz.explain: Email HTML is rendered by the recipient's mail client, not by the app's browser context. CSS custom properties defined via :root or [data-theme] are not available. The comment in lib/ppr-agency-notify.ts explicitly states 'email HTML uses literal hex colors on purpose — recipient mail clients can't resolve CSS variables, so emails (like PDFs) stay theme-independent.'

title: Shift Checklist
summary: The Shift Checklist module provides a per-day, three-shift task list with a 3-state toggle (unchecked / completed / N/A), configurable 0600L reset, and per-item attribution stamped at completion time.
concept:
The Shift Checklist is the AMOPS daily task tracker — a structured list of items that must be worked through each shift, filed once swing shift completes all items, and then reset for the next operational day. Unlike inspections (one-per-day hard lock), the shift checklist is one-per-date: it is shared across all users at a base and reflects the collective completion state of the day's tasks.

Data model

  • shift_checklist_items — template rows defining what must be done. Each item has a shift ('day' | 'swing' | 'mid'), frequency ('daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly'), and sort_order.
  • shift_checklists — one row per (base_id, checklist_date). status is 'in_progress' or 'completed'. Created lazily by fetchOrCreateTodayChecklist() on first load.
  • shift_checklist_responses — one row per (checklist_id, item_id). Upserted on every toggle via a (checklist_id, item_id) unique constraint. Stores completed, is_na, completed_by, and completed_at.

3-state toggle cycle

Tapping an item cycles through: unchecked → completed (green checkmark) → N/A (grey "N/A" label) → unchecked. The transition logic in handleToggle reads the current completed + is_na booleans and computes the next state. Both completed = true and is_na = true count as "done" for the purposes of the shift section progress counter and the "File Checklist" unlock condition.

Timezone-aware reset — 0600L

getEffectiveChecklistDate(timezone, resetTime) in lib/supabase/shift-checklist.ts computes today's checklist date in the base's IANA timezone. If the current wall-clock time is before the checklist_reset_time stored on bases (default '06:00'), the effective date is yesterday — so a mid-shift Airfield Manager working at 0300L is still working on yesterday's checklist, not a new one. This matches the same pattern used by inspections and daily reviews.

Frequency filtering

itemAppliesToday(item, timezone, resetTime) filters the template items to only those that apply on the effective date: daily items always appear, weekly items appear on Mondays, monthly items appear on the 1st of the month.

Attribution stamping

Every upsertResponse call captures the current user id via auth.getUser() and writes it to completed_by (with completed_at ISO timestamp) when the item is actioned. The page resolves these ids to "Rank Name" strings via a batch profiles lookup and renders them as a sub-label under each completed item.

Filing the checklist

Once all items are done (every item has a completed or is_na response), the "File Checklist" button becomes active. Filing calls completeChecklist(), which sets status = 'completed', completed_by, and completed_at. A filed checklist is locked — toggles are disabled — but can be reopened if needed.

code.caption: getEffectiveChecklistDate — timezone-aware 0600L reset
code.from: export function getEffectiveChecklistDate(timezone: string, resetTime: string): string {
code.note: Converts wall-clock now to the base timezone, compares against the reset time, and rolls back one calendar day if before reset.

annotations.note: If current wall-clock minutes < reset minutes, setDate(-1) rolls back to yesterday's checklist date.

code.caption: upsertResponse — 3-state response write with attribution
code.note: Upserts on the (checklist_id, item_id) unique constraint. completed_by and completed_at are cleared when un-checking.

annotations.note: isActioned gate: completed_by and completed_at are only written when the item is being checked or marked N/A, not when un-checking.

code.caption: handleToggle — 3-state cycle and optimistic update
code.note: Reads current completed + is_na booleans to compute the next cycle state, then writes via upsertResponse.

annotations.note: The three-state cycle: unchecked → nextCompleted=true, completed → nextNa=true, N/A → both false (back to unchecked).

fits: The Shift Checklist page uses useInstallation() to get both installationId and the base's timezone / checklist_reset_time. The page is a client component that calls fetchOrCreateTodayChecklist() on load — this creates the shift_checklists row lazily, so there is no migration-time seed required. Items are split into Day / Swing / Mid sections using simple array filters on the shift column. The History tab fetches the 30 most recent shift_checklists rows and lets users drill into any completed checklist in read-only mode.

quiz.q: A base's checklist_reset_time is '06:00' and timezone is 'America/Chicago'. An Airfield Manager logs in at 0430 local time. Which checklist date does fetchOrCreateTodayChecklist return?
choices: Today's calendar date in UTC
Today's calendar date in America/Chicago
Yesterday's calendar date in America/Chicago, because 0430 is before the 0600 reset
The last filed checklist date, regardless of the current time
quiz.explain: getEffectiveChecklistDate converts now to America/Chicago local time (0430) and compares it against resetTime (0600). Because 0430 < 0600, local.setDate(-1) rolls back one day. The mid-shift Airfield Manager is still working yesterday's operational day.

quiz.q: An item currently has completed = true, is_na = false. The Airfield Manager taps it. What is the resulting state?
choices: completed = false, is_na = false (back to unchecked)
completed = false, is_na = true (N/A)
completed = true, is_na = true (both)
The item is locked because it was already completed
quiz.explain: The 3-state cycle in handleToggle: when currentCompleted = true and currentNa = false, the code sets nextNa = true (nextCompleted stays false). This moves the item from completed to N/A. The next tap would set both to false (unchecked).

quiz.q: A weekly checklist item has frequency = 'weekly'. On which days does itemAppliesToday return true?
choices: Every day — the 'weekly' tag is cosmetic only
Mondays only, based on the effective date in the base timezone
The first weekday of each week, computed by counting ISO week numbers
Sundays only, because getDay() === 0 is checked
quiz.explain: itemAppliesToday in lib/supabase/shift-checklist.ts checks d.getDay() === 1 for weekly items (1 = Monday). The date object is built from the effective checklist date string at noon to avoid DST edge cases.

title: Daily Reviews
summary: The Daily Reviews module implements DAFMAN 13-204v1 Para 2.5.2.10.3 and 10.4 shift sign-off: per-date rows with up to 5 signing slots, an events hash for AMENDED detection, and a PDF preview generated at sign time.
concept:
DAFMAN 13-204v1 Paragraph 2.5.2.10.3 and 10.4 require that the Daily Operations events for each date be certified by the Airfield Manager, the Night Airfield Manager/Operations (NAMO), and the AMSL for each shift. Glidepath implements this requirement with the daily_reviews table — one row per (base_id, review_date) — and a sign-off flow that generates and displays the Daily Ops PDF inline so the signer can review exactly what they are certifying before signing. A T-3 waiver is on file substituting this web-based program for the CAC-signed AF Form 3616 requirement.

Signing slots

Each daily_reviews row has five potential signing slots:

Slot keyUSAF labelPermission
day_amslDay Shift AMSLdaily_reviews:sign:amsl
swing_amslSwing Shift AMSLdaily_reviews:sign:amsl
mid_amslMid Shift AMSLdaily_reviews:sign:amsl
namoNAMOdaily_reviews:sign:namo
afmAirfield Managerdaily_reviews:sign:afm

Two-shift bases require day_amsl + swing_amsl + namo + afm; three-shift bases additionally require mid_amsl. requiredSlotsForShifts(shiftCount) computes the required set. isFullyCertified(row, shiftCount) checks that every required slot has a non-null _signed_at timestamp.

Civilian mode labels

Slot labels are mode-aware: getSlotLabel(slot, base) calls getTerm(SLOT_TO_TERM[slot], base) from lib/airport-mode.ts. On a civilian Part 139 airport, day_amsl becomes "Day Shift Lead", namo becomes "Ops Supervisor", and afm becomes "Operations Manager".

Events hash and AMENDED detection

At sign time, the sign modal fetches all Daily Ops events for the review date (using the base's local timezone window) and computes a SHA-256 hash of the sorted entity IDs. This hash is stored in {slot}_events_hash. If the Events Log shows entries whose created_at is after the row's fully_certified_at, the activity log renders an "AMENDED" pill — indicating that new events were logged after the review was certified.

Timezone window precision

getReviewWindowUtc(reviewDate, timezone, resetTime) computes the exact UTC range for a review date: from reviewDate + resetTime in the base's local timezone to reviewDate+1 + resetTime. This uses Intl.DateTimeFormat for wall-clock-to-UTC conversion, which is independent of the Vercel server's UTC timezone. The same reset-time boundary logic is shared with shift-checklist and inspections.

Offline write queue integration

Signing goes through the write queue (getWriteQueue().enqueueOrExecute). If the network is unavailable, the sign action is queued as a 'daily_review_sign' operation and committed when connectivity returns. The page listens for WRITE_COMMITTED_EVENT to re-fetch when a queued sign drains.

PDF preview at sign time

The sign modal generates the Daily Ops PDF inline via generateDailyOpsPdf() (from lib/reports/daily-ops-pdf.ts) with the current review sign-off state. On iOS/iPadOS the PDF blob URL is replaced with a "open in native viewer" link, because WebKit only renders the first page of a PDF in an <iframe>.

code.caption: getReviewWindowUtc — precise UTC window using Intl.DateTimeFormat
code.note: Converts the base's local reset time to UTC using Intl.DateTimeFormat so the window is correct on Vercel (UTC) and developer machines alike.

code.caption: signDailyReview — upsert pattern and fully_certified_at auto-fire
code.note: Upserts the daily_reviews row (insert if none exists for the date, update otherwise). Sets fully_certified_at atomically once all required slots are filled.

annotations.note: fully_certified_at is set in a second UPDATE only after isFullyCertified() confirms all required slots — avoids a second DB round-trip when not needed.

code.caption: computeEventsHash — SHA-256 of sorted entity IDs for AMENDED detection
code.from: export async function computeEventsHash(ids: string[]): Promise<string> {
code.note: Uses crypto.subtle (Web Crypto API) when available; falls back to a deterministic FNV-like hash for environments where crypto.subtle is absent.

code.caption: handleSign — offline-queue integration and PDF regeneration after sign
code.note: Signs via the write queue (works offline), shows a 'queued' toast if offline, regenerates the PDF preview with updated signer info, and auto-opens the email modal on full certification.

fits: Daily Reviews integrates with the useInstallation() context for shift_count, timezone, and checklist_reset_time. The page renders 14 days back from today (using getEffectiveReviewDate to anchor "today" in the base's local timezone) as a scrollable list, each row showing slot tiles that go green when signed. Clicking any row opens the DailyReviewSignModal. The permission matrix gates each signing slot separately: AMSL, NAMO, and Airfield Manager roles have distinct daily_reviews:sign:* permission keys.

quiz.q: A base is in 'America/Denver' with a reset time of '06:00'. An Airfield Manager signs the swing_amsl slot at 0230L (Mountain Time). What review_date is written to the daily_reviews row?
choices: Today's calendar date in UTC
Today's calendar date in America/Denver, because it is still the same calendar day
Yesterday's calendar date in America/Denver, because 0230 is before the 0600 reset
The date returned by new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)
quiz.explain: getEffectiveReviewDate uses the base's timezone and reset time. At 0230 Mountain Time, currentMinutes (150) < resetMinutes (360), so the date is rolled back one day. The Airfield Manager is still on the previous operational day's review, matching the shift-checklist behavior.

quiz.q: A daily_reviews row has fully_certified_at = '2026-06-04T18:00:00Z'. Later that day, a new discrepancy is logged at 19:00Z. What UI indicator appears in the Events Log for that date?
choices: A red dot indicating that re-certification is required
An AMENDED pill next to entries whose created_at is after fully_certified_at
The row is automatically re-opened and all slots are cleared
Nothing — fully_certified_at is immutable once set
quiz.explain: The Events Log renders an 'AMENDED' pill when a log entry's created_at is after the review row's fully_certified_at. This surfaces that new events were recorded after the daily review was certified, prompting the Airfield Manager to review the amendment.

quiz.q: A civilian Part 139 airport has airport_type = 'faa_part139'. What label does the sign modal show for the 'namo' slot?
choices: NAMO — the slot key is always displayed directly
Night Airfield Manager Operations
Ops Supervisor, because getSlotLabel calls getTerm('shift_supervisor', base) which returns the civilian equivalent
Operations Manager
quiz.explain: getSlotLabel(slot, base) calls getTerm(SLOT_TO_TERM[slot], base). SLOT_TO_TERM maps 'namo' → 'shift_supervisor'. getTerm('shift_supervisor', base) returns 'Ops Supervisor' for civilian Part 139 bases per the dual-mode terminology framework in lib/airport-mode.ts.

title: SCN — Secondary Crash Net
summary: The SCN module logs daily and monthly back-up Secondary Crash Net radio checks with per-agency response statuses (Loud & Clear, No Response, Out of Service), writes a summary to the Events Log, and generates a monthly compliance PDF.
concept:
The Secondary Crash Net (SCN) is the backup radio network used when the primary crash net fails during an aircraft emergency. DAFMAN requires daily primary SCN checks and a monthly back-up SCN check. The Glidepath SCN module provides a structured log of these checks, recording the response status of every configured agency for each check event.

Data model

  • scn_agencies — the roster of agencies that participate in the SCN check at a base (e.g. Fire Department, ATC, Security Forces). Managed in Base Setup. Ordered by sort_order.
  • scn_checks — one row per check event. check_type is 'primary' (daily) or 'backup' (monthly). Keyed by (base_id, check_date, check_type) — only one primary and one backup check per Zulu day. check_date is a Zulu YYYY-MM-DD string.
  • scn_check_results — one row per (check_id, agency). status is 'loud_clear', 'no_response', or 'oos' (out of service). OOS entries require a notes value — the UI enforces this before saving.

Upsert-on-resubmit

saveCheck() first looks up an existing check row by (base_id, check_date, check_type). If one exists, it updates the check row and deletes + re-inserts all result rows. This means an Airfield Manager can correct a check submission for the same Zulu date without creating a duplicate — the check is simply overwritten.

Three agency status values

ValueLabelColor
loud_clearLoud & Clearsuccess (green)
no_responseNo Responsewarning (amber)
oosOut of Servicedanger (red)

When an Airfield Manager sets an agency to Out of Service, the modal auto-opens an inline notes dialog for that agency — saving is blocked until the OOS agency has a notes entry.

Events Log summary

summarizeCheck() in lib/supabase/scn.ts generates a human-readable summary string. If all agencies are Loud & Clear: "Daily SCN check complete — all agencies loud & clear". If any exceptions exist, they are listed: "...all loud & clear except Fire Dept (No Response), ATC (Out of Service: radio fault)". This string is written to activity_log via logActivity() with entity type 'scn' or 'scn_backup'.

Monthly compliance PDF

generateScnMonthlyPdf() in lib/scn-pdf.ts generates a matrix PDF with dates as rows and agencies as columns. The export is triggered from the "Monthly Report" panel on the SCN page, which uses an <input type="month"> to select the month and fetches checks via fetchChecksInRange().

code.caption: saveCheck — upsert-on-resubmit with result row delete+reinsert
code.note: Shows the full upsert pattern: look up existing by (base_id, check_date, check_type), update+clear results if found, insert new if not. Activity log written with summarizeCheck().

annotations.note: If an existing check is found, it is updated and scn_check_results are deleted before re-inserting — prevents duplicate rows from a correction.
Activity log written with uppercase details string, using entity type 'scn_backup' for monthly checks and 'scn' for daily.

code.caption: summarizeCheck — human-readable Events Log entry builder
code.note: All-clear vs exception path. OOS notes are appended inline: '(Out of Service: radio fault)' format.

code.caption: openModal + setAgencyStatus — OOS notes enforcement
code.note: Builds the agency draft array from configured agencies (with prior check values if editing). setAgencyStatus auto-opens the OOS notes dialog when switching to 'oos'.

annotations.note: When an agency is set to 'oos' and has no notes yet, oosDialog is set to { idx } — opening the inline notes input for that agency row.

fits: The SCN page loads agencies via fetchScnAgencies(installationId, true) (active only) and today's checks via fetchTodayChecks(installationId). If no agencies are configured, an inline warning directs admins to Base Setup. Check dates use Zulu time (todayZuluDate() = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)) matching the rest of the app's Zulu convention. The 30-day history panel filters out today's checks so they appear only in the Today section. Operating initials are fetched once on mount from the user's profile and passed to saveCheck() for attribution in the check record.

quiz.q: An Airfield Manager submits the daily SCN check but entered Fire Dept status as 'loud_clear' by mistake. They resubmit with 'no_response'. How many scn_checks rows exist after the correction?
choices: Two rows — one for each submission
One row — saveCheck upserts on (base_id, check_date, check_type) and clears/re-inserts results
One row for the check and two scn_check_results rows for Fire Dept
The correction is rejected because the check is already completed
quiz.explain: saveCheck() queries for an existing row matching (base_id, check_date, check_type). If found, it updates the check row and deletes all scn_check_results for that check_id before re-inserting. This makes corrections idempotent — only one check row per type per Zulu day.

quiz.q: The Airfield Manager sets ATC to 'oos' (Out of Service) in the SCN check modal but clicks Save without entering OOS notes. What happens?
choices: The check is saved with a blank notes field for ATC
The Save button is disabled until OOS notes are entered
handleSaveModal checks for any OOS row with empty notes and calls toast.error, blocking the save
The ATC row is automatically removed from the check submission
quiz.explain: handleSaveModal in app/(app)/scn/page.tsx has a pre-save guard: it calls modal.draft.find(d => d.status === 'oos' && !d.notes.trim()). If any OOS agency has no notes, it calls toast.error and returns early without calling saveCheck.

quiz.q: A base has 4 agencies: Fire, ATC, Security Forces, and Command Post. The daily SCN check shows Fire = loud_clear, ATC = no_response, Security Forces = loud_clear, Command Post = oos (notes: 'radio maintenance'). What does the Events Log entry say?
choices: Daily SCN check complete — all agencies loud & clear
Daily SCN check complete — all loud & clear except ATC (No Response), Command Post (Out of Service: radio maintenance)
Daily SCN check complete — 2 exceptions: ATC, Command Post
Daily SCN check complete — ATC No Response; Command Post OOS
quiz.explain: summarizeCheck filters for results where status !== 'loud_clear'. For each exception it formats '${agency_name} (${SCN_STATUS_LABELS[status]}${notes ? ': ' + notes : ''})'. ATC has no notes so it's '(No Response)'. Command Post has notes so it's '(Out of Service: radio maintenance)'.

title: Contractors / Personnel on Airfield
summary: The Contractors module tracks personnel and companies operating on the airfield, including AF Form 483 escort credential numbers with expiry dates, contact details, radio/flag/callsign assignment, and template-based quick entry for repeat contractors.
concept:
Any person or company working on the airfield — construction crews, fuel truck operators, maintenance contractors — must be tracked by AMOPS with their escort credential (AF Form 483 on USAF bases; SIDA badge on civilian airports). Glidepath's Personnel on Airfield module provides a log of active and historical contractor entries with expiry-date tracking for credentials and a PDF export for shift briefing.

Data model

All contractor entries live in the airfield_contractors table. Key fields:

FieldPurpose
company_namePrimary identifier (required)
callsignRadio callsign, used as the short identifier in activity log entries if set
locationWhere on the airfield the work is occurring (required)
work_descriptionNature of the work (required)
status'active' or 'completed'
af_form_483Credential number (mode-aware label: AF Form 483 on USAF, SIDA badge on civilian)
af_form_483_expirationCredential expiry date — the UI can surface expiry warnings
radio_number / flag_numberAssignment tracking for issued AMOPS resources

Mode-aware credential label

getTerm('form_483', currentInstallation) from lib/airport-mode.ts returns the appropriate credential label. On USAF airfields it returns "AF Form 483"; on a civilian Part 139 airport it returns the civilian equivalent. This keeps the module title and section headers as "Personnel on Airfield" while the specific credential field uses the correct terminology.

Activity log integration

createContractor() writes a logged_personnel activity event with detail "CALLSIGN ON AIRFIELD FOR WORK_DESCRIPTION" (callsign falling back to company name if not set). updateContractor() writes personnel_off_airfield when status → completed or a generic updated event for other field changes. All detail strings are uppercased.

Contractor templates

Repeat contractors (fuel trucks, regular maintenance crews) can be saved as templates stored in the bases.contractor_templates JSONB column. Templates snapshot company name, contact, callsign, credential number, and expiry. The save path uses a read-then-write pattern (refreshAndGetTemplates() fetches the latest DB state before appending) to prevent concurrent saves from overwriting each other's templates.

Auto-complete on status change

When updateContractor(id, { status: 'completed' }) is called, the code automatically sets end_date to today if no explicit end date was provided. This ensures the completed contractor record has an accurate off-airfield date without requiring the Airfield Manager to manually fill it in.

PDF export

The personnel PDF is generated by lib/personnel-pdf.ts via generatePersonnelPdf(), taking the currently filtered + searched contractor list. It can be saved locally or emailed via the EmailPdfModal. The PDF title reflects the active filter tab (Active / All / Completed).

code.caption: createContractor — activity log on creation, callsign-or-company fallback
code.note: Shows the full create path including optional base_id + created_by, and the logActivity call with uppercase detail string.

annotations.note: Activity detail: callsign preferred over company_name as the short identifier; work_description included in the log.

code.caption: updateContractor — status completion, auto end_date, activity log branching
code.note: Auto-sets end_date when status → completed; logs 'personnel_off_airfield' vs 'updated' based on the nature of the change.

code.caption: Template read-then-write — concurrent save protection
code.note: saveTemplates writes the full template array to bases.contractor_templates. refreshAndGetTemplates fetches fresh state first to prevent overwrite races.

fits: The Contractors page uses useInstallation() for both installationId and currentInstallation (needed for getTerm('form_483', ...)). Filtering is done client-side — the page fetches all contractors for the base and applies filter/search in memory. The DEMO_CONTRACTORS array in lib/demo-data.ts provides demo-mode data when Supabase is not configured. The CONTRACTOR_STATUS_CONFIG from lib/constants.ts provides display labels and colors for the active/completed status badges.

quiz.q: An Airfield Manager marks a contractor entry as 'completed' without specifying an end_date. What happens to end_date in the database?
choices: end_date remains null — the caller must provide it explicitly
end_date is set to the contractor's start_date as a default
updateContractor automatically sets end_date to today's date if no end_date was provided in the fields object
The status update is rejected until end_date is supplied
quiz.explain: updateContractor in lib/supabase/contractors.ts has an auto-set: 'if (fields.status === 'completed' && !fields.end_date) { updateFields.end_date = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0] }'. This silently fills in today's date so the record always has an accurate off-airfield date.

quiz.q: A contractor has callsign = 'REACH77' and company_name = 'Boeing'. What text appears in the Events Log when the contractor is created?
choices: Boeing ON AIRFIELD FOR WORK_DESCRIPTION
REACH77 ON AIRFIELD FOR WORK_DESCRIPTION
CONTRACTOR ADDED: REACH77 (Boeing)
logged_personnel: Boeing / REACH77
quiz.explain: createContractor uses 'const label = (input.callsign || input.company_name).toUpperCase()' — callsign takes priority over company_name. The activity detail is `${label} ON AIRFIELD FOR ${work_description.toUpperCase()}`.

quiz.q: A base's airport_type is 'faa_part139'. What does the AF Form 483 field label read in the contractor form?
choices: AF Form 483 — the label is hardcoded in the form component
SIDA Badge or the civilian equivalent returned by getTerm('form_483', currentInstallation)
Escort Credential — a generic fallback used for all airport types
The field is hidden entirely on civilian Part 139 airports
quiz.explain: ContractorsPage calls 'const credentialLabel = getTerm('form_483', currentInstallation)' from lib/airport-mode.ts. The USAF mode returns 'AF Form 483'; the civilian Part 139 mode returns the appropriate civilian credential label. The module title stays 'Personnel on Airfield' regardless.
